snapshot.scope: Independent Product Planning review binds public_only URL capture to canonical hostnames and fresh fail-closed Fake-IP checks: target and example.com probe each require benchmark IPv4 and only strict Fake-IP forms; connections pin target IPv4, while literal, mixed, IPv6-only, unconfirmed, localhost and private targets still block with existing per-hop, bound, redaction and inert-content controls.

reviewers.task: Public URL system DNS Fake-IP compatibility review
reviewers.independence: Product Planning owns only documentation and independently compares the Development-only Source Fetch and focused test bytes with security, parser, transport, trace and test contracts.

dimensions.summary: Source Fetch remains the sole transport owner; each blocked target and redirect gets a fresh hostname-only probe, adding no renderer, storage, Job, permission or secret owner.
Security owns the full Fake-IP predicate; Parser, Architecture, Quality and Decision projections agree that benchmark space is not reclassified and text/markdown stays inert.
PIGE-PI-003, B5.02 and existing partial E5.01 evidence reflect the bounded compatibility rule without promoting any Requirement, Build, Exit, Phase or release status.
Focused Source Fetch tests cover fresh target/probe checks, required and pinned IPv4, inert Markdown, plus literal, mixed, IPv6-only, unconfirmed, private and redirect failures.
One security owner holds the full rule; short projections and compact trace updates keep governed Markdown and trace manifests within their exact byte budgets.

verification.command: Independent review of public URL system DNS Fake-IP compatibility
verification.evidence: Review confirms every blocked target and redirect gets a fresh probe; target and probe require strict Fake-IP plus IPv4, only validated target IPv4 is pinned, and all other fetch controls remain.

verification.command: npm run verify:docs && npm run verify:decisions && npm run verify:traceability
verification.evidence: Links, whitespace, document map, PRD structure, decisions and locked claims pass with no Requirement, Build, Exit or Phase promotion.

verification.command: npm run verify:contracts && npm run verify:independent-documentation-review && npm run verify:documentation-leanness
verification.evidence: CON-001 through CON-004 remain green; focused UI tests, typecheck, build and visual-contract verification bind the exact repair while the union recipe retains the published Reader, Update, release and security roots.

coordination.taskTitle: Public URL system DNS Fake-IP compatibility
coordination.compatibility: The candidate changes only public_only Source Fetch handling for confirmed system Fake-IP hostnames and text/markdown; no renderer, IPC, persistence, secret, Job, model, destructive, permission or filesystem authority changes.
coordination.acknowledgement: Product Planning records the bounded compatibility boundary and keeps Development code/test ownership separate, with no broad status promotion.

nonBlockingRisks: A compromised system DNS/proxy can still redirect public hostnames; all-address confirmation, independent probing, per-hop revalidation and connection pinning are retained defenses, not proof of remote ownership.
No Requirement, Build, Exit or Phase status is promoted; broader bypass coverage and signed-platform proxy/DNS evidence remain open.
